retry_reason: Failure (e.g., memory allocation failure in __aa_create_ns) is not transient in the sense that immediate retry is unlikely to succeed; callers typically propagate -ENOMEM instead of retrying.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper: finds an existing namespace by name and increments its reference count via aa_get_ns, or creates a new namespace with an initial reference via __aa_create_ns (which sets refcount to 1). The function returns the namespace pointer with a reference that the caller must release via aa_put_ns when done. Locking of the parent namespace protects the lookup and creation. On failure (e.g., creation out of memory), returns NULL; the caller must check the return value.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference to a namespace (by find+get or create+set) and return it to the caller. The only non-refcount logic is the mutex lock/unlock and the name lookup, which are incidental to the get-or-create pattern.
judge.retry_reason: Failure (e.g., memory allocation failure in __aa_create_ns) is not transient in the sense that immediate retry is unlikely to succeed; callers typically propagate -ENOMEM instead of retrying.

check.reason: aa_get_ns() increments refcount on the returned ns. Access path ns.unconfined.label.count.count.refcount and type chain aa_ns--aa_profile--aa_label--aa_count--kref--refcount_t are consistent with code and callee info. Critical variable is the return value (index 0).
